mnemonics.title: RIBL - Tree Components
mnemonics.content: Root (first split), Internal nodes (feature tests), Branches (outcomes), Leaves (predictions) - the four parts of every decision tree.

mnemonics.title: Tree = Flowchart
mnemonics.content: Think of a decision tree as a flowchart: each question (node) leads to branches, and the final answer is at the leaves.

mnemonics.title: White Box vs Black Box
mnemonics.content: Decision Trees = White box (transparent, readable rules). Neural Networks = Black box (hard to interpret). Trees win on explainability.

mnemonics.title: CART = Both Ways
mnemonics.content: Classification AND Regression Trees. One model type handles discrete labels (classification) and continuous values (regression).

mnemonics.title: Divide and Conquer
mnemonics.content: Split data using best feature -> create purer subsets -> repeat until pure or stopping criterion. That is decision tree learning in a nutshell.
